hero.tagline: Run Docker containers on your phone. One binary, zero dependencies, no root required.

features.label: Features
features.subtext: Docker cannot run on Android. Doki can. Same OCI images, same workflow, fraction of the resources.
features.zeroDepsDesc: Single static binary. No containerd, no runc, no libseccomp, no systemd. Just copy and run. Ships everything you need in one ~8.5MB executable.
features.ociPodmanDesc: Full Docker Hub support. 39 Podman API v5 endpoints. Same API as Docker Engine v1.54. Your docker-compose files work out of the box.
features.kubernetesDesc: 6 components: API Server, Kubelet, Scheduler, 10 Controllers, Kube-proxy, CoreDNS. 80 K8s API types. Full kubectl client.
features.nineBinariesDesc: doki, dokid, doki-compose, doki-init, doki-kube, doki-kubectl. All in one package. ARM64, ARMv7, macOS native.
features.macosNativeDesc: Apple Virtualization.framework for macOS 11+. QEMU fallback for Intel Macs. Sandbox backend for lightweight isolation.
features.meshDesc: Peer-to-peer container networking. mDNS auto-discovery. Encrypted with TLS 1.3 + secretbox. Zero config.
features.landlockDesc: Linux 5.13+ ABI v9 support. Filesystem, network, scope rules. Auto-detection with fallback. Secure by default.
features.rootlessDesc: Runs as unprivileged user on Android. No root, no namespace tricks. Just works.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.headline: Pull, build, run.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Download the single binary and install with one command. No dependencies, no daemon, no systemd.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Pull any OCI image from Docker Hub or any registry. Multi-arch, layer caching, content-addressable storage.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Run containers with the same CLI you already know. Port mapping, volumes, networks, environment variables.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Inspect, stop, restart, and remove containers. Compose, Kube, daemon mode. All included.

terminal.title: Try it
terminal.subtitle: See it in action.
terminal.desc: Pull an image, run a container, map a port. All in a few seconds.

builtWith.label: Built with
builtWith.headline: Go, C, and a little ObjC.
builtWith.subtext: 55,000+ lines of Go. 16,000 lines of C. 1,000 lines of Objective-C for the macOS VZ backend.

dokiVsDocker.label: Performance
dokiVsDocker.headline: Docker vs Doki.
dokiVsDocker.subtext: Docker cannot run on Android. Doki was built for it from the ground up.
dokiVsDocker.footnote: Benchmarks measured on Pixel 8 (Android 14). Docker measured on Ubuntu 24.04 (x86_64). Your mileage may vary.

mesh.headline: Mesh. Kubernetes. macOS native.
mesh.subtext: Everything you need to run containers anywhere.
mesh.kubeDesc: API Server, Kubelet, Scheduler, 10 Controllers, Kube-proxy, CoreDNS. 80 API types. All built in.
mesh.meshDesc: mDNS discovery, TLS 1.3 + secretbox encryption, zero-config setup. Connect devices anywhere.
mesh.macosDesc: Native ARM64 performance via Virtualization.framework. No Docker Desktop, no VM overhead.
mesh.androidDesc: Runs in Termux. Proot-based isolation. No root, no bootloader unlock. Just works.

testimonials.title: Community
testimonials.headline: What developers are saying.
testimonials.clickHint: Click on a node to explore

cta.headline: Run containers on your phone.
cta.subtext: One binary. Zero dependencies. No root.

footer.brandTagline: The universal container engine. Runs on Android, Linux, and macOS.

install.title: Install Doki
install.subtitle: One binary. Zero dependencies. Run anywhere.

dokiCli.subtext: One binary. Zero dependencies. No root. Run any OCI image on Android, Linux, or macOS — with the same CLI you already know.
dokiCli.ctaSubtext: One curl command and you are up and running.
dokiCli.featuresTitle: Everything you need to run containers.
dokiCli.feat1Desc: Pull, run, and manage any image from Docker Hub or any OCI registry. Full layer caching, multi-arch support.
dokiCli.feat2Desc: 39 endpoints — same API as Docker Engine v1.54. Works with docker-compose, Podman clients, and existing tooling.
dokiCli.feat3Desc: Single 8.56MB static binary. No containerd, no runc, no libseccomp. Copy it to any Linux, Android, or macOS machine.
dokiCli.feat4Desc: From WASM sandbox to microVM. Doki probes the host and picks the strongest available mode automatically.
dokiCli.feat5Desc: Runs as unprivileged user. No root, no sudo, no namespace tricks. Works on stock Android phones.
dokiCli.feat6Desc: Internal DNS server with automatic container resolution. Custom upstream DNS, port 8053 on Android.

dokiCompose.subtext: Your docker-compose.yml files work as-is. Doki Compose is a drop-in replacement that runs on Android, Linux, and macOS.
dokiCompose.feat1Desc: Reads existing docker-compose.yml files. No rewriting needed. Volumes, networks, ports, env vars — all work.
dokiCompose.feat2Desc: Start, stop, scale, and manage groups of containers. Dependency ordering, health checks, restart policies.
dokiCompose.feat3Desc: Each compose project gets its own network namespace, volume namespace, and lifecycle. No cross-project interference.
dokiCompose.feat4Desc: Watch for file changes and automatically rebuild containers. Perfect for local development workflows.
dokiCompose.feat5Desc: Mix ARM64 and AMD64 images. Doki handles platform emulation transparently on all supported architectures.
dokiCompose.feat6Desc: Manage sensitive data with built-in secrets support. Mount configs without baking them into images.

dokiKube.subtext: A full Kubernetes cluster in a ~15MB binary. Run K8s on your Android phone, Raspberry Pi, or macOS laptop.
dokiKube.featuresTitle: The full K8s API. No infrastructure required.
dokiKube.feat1Desc: API Server, Kubelet, Scheduler, 10 Controllers, Kube-proxy, CoreDNS. All built in — no external dependencies.
dokiKube.feat2Desc: Pods, Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps, Secrets, Ingress, CRDs, RBAC, and more. Full kubectl compatibility.
dokiKube.feat3Desc: doki-kube starts a complete K8s control plane in seconds. No etcd, no containerd, no CNI plugins required.
dokiKube.feat4Desc: doki-kubectl is a bundled kubectl binary configured to talk to your local cluster. No version mismatches.
dokiKube.feat5Desc: Run a K8s cluster on your phone, laptop, or edge device. Same API, same manifests, same tooling.
dokiKube.feat6Desc: ~15MB for the complete control plane + kubectl. Fraction of the memory and disk of a standard K8s deployment.

dokid.subtext: Persistent container daemon with a full Podman-compatible API. Run it on a server, a Raspberry Pi, or your Android phone.
dokid.feat1Desc: dokid runs as a persistent daemon, managing container lifecycles, networking, and image storage. Start it once and forget it.
dokid.feat2Desc: Exposes 39 Podman API v5 endpoints over HTTP. Connect any Podman-compatible client, including docker CLI and docker-compose.
dokid.feat3Desc: Listen on Unix socket (secured) or TCP (remote access). Configurable via DOKID_HOST environment variable.
dokid.feat4Desc: Pull, tag, push, and prune images. Layer caching with content-addressable storage. Multi-arch support.
dokid.feat5Desc: Create, inspect, and manage volumes. Mount paths, named volumes, tmpfs. Bind-mount support for host paths.
dokid.feat6Desc: Health checks, restart policies, resource limits (CPU, memory, PIDs). Graceful shutdown and timeout controls.

android.subtext: Run Docker containers on your phone or tablet. No root, no custom ROM, no compromises. Doki was built for Android from the ground up.
android.feat1Desc: Doki runs entirely within Termux. No cross-compilation, no chroot, no VNC. Just install and run.
android.feat2Desc: Uses proot-based isolation that works on stock Android. No bootloader unlock, no Magisk, no kernel mods.
android.feat3Desc: DNS on port 8053, automatic LD_PRELOAD stripping, and proot execve fallback handle SELinux restrictions transparently.
android.feat4Desc: Native ARM64 builds for modern phones. ARMv7 for older devices and Chromebooks in Android mode.
android.feat5Desc: Pull any image from Docker Hub. Run nginx, PostgreSQL, Redis, Python, Node.js — anything in the registry.
android.feat6Desc: dokid runs as a persistent service. Containers stay alive after closing Termux. Manage via the API from any app.

linux.subtext: Lightweight container runtime for servers, desktops, and embedded Linux. One binary replaces containerd, runc, and Docker Engine.
linux.feat1Desc: No containerd, runc, dockerd, or systemd. One ~8.5MB static binary with zero runtime dependencies.
linux.feat2Desc: doki-kube starts a full K8s control plane. Perfect for local development, CI, or edge clusters.
linux.feat3Desc: Auto-probes the host and picks the strongest mode: native, proot, Landlock, or microVM.
linux.feat4Desc: Use docker CLI, docker-compose, or any Podman client. Full API compatibility.
linux.feat5Desc: No sudo, no setuid, no capabilities required. Runs as an unprivileged user.
linux.feat6Desc: 12MB idle memory, 8.56MB binary. Ideal for resource-constrained VPS and Raspberry Pi.

macOS.subtext: Native Apple Silicon support with Apple Virtualization.framework. No Docker Desktop, no Orbstack, no VM overhead.
macOS.feat1Desc: Uses Apple Virtualization.framework on macOS 11+. Native ARM64 performance without emulation overhead.
macOS.feat2Desc: Doki replaces Docker Desktop entirely. No VM, no HyperKit, no license fees. Just a single binary.
macOS.feat3Desc: Intel Macs fall back to QEMU automatically. Same API, same workflow, same CLI.
macOS.feat4Desc: Connect from docker CLI, Podman, or any API client. Full compatibility with existing tooling.
macOS.feat5Desc: doki-kube runs a full K8s cluster on macOS. Perfect for local development before deploying to production.
macOS.feat6Desc: Optional sandbox backend for lightweight process isolation. Great for development and testing workflows.

ciCd.subtext: Ephemeral container environments for CI pipelines. Spin up, run tests, tear down. No daemon required.
ciCd.feat1Desc: Doki runs containers directly. No dockerd, no containerd, no background processes. Perfect for ephemeral runners.
ciCd.feat2Desc: First container starts in milliseconds. No waiting for a daemon to initialize.
ciCd.feat3Desc: No state to clean up. Containers are removed when the process exits. No dangling resources.
ciCd.feat4Desc: doki-kube starts a full K8s cluster in CI. Test manifests, run e2e tests, then destroy.
ciCd.feat5Desc: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, CircleCI — Doki runs anywhere a binary can execute.
ciCd.feat6Desc: 8.56MB binary. Layer caching avoids re-pulling images. Fraction of the storage of Docker in CI.

security.subtext: Doki applies security at every layer: seccomp, capabilities, user namespaces, Landlock, mTLS, and image verification. Rootless by default, safe by design.
security.layer1Desc: Default profile allows ~80 syscalls including io_uring, pidfd, rseq, userfaultfd, landlock. Blocks ~250+ unnecessary syscalls.
security.layer2Desc: Drops all capabilities by default. Opt-in via --cap-add for specific use cases. No NET_RAW, no SYS_ADMIN without explicit request.
security.layer3Desc: Root inside container maps to unprivileged user outside. No uid 0 on the host. Kernel-enforced isolation.
security.layer4Desc: Linux 5.13+ ABI v9. Filesystem sandbox, network restrictions, scope rules. Auto-detection with graceful fallback.
security.layer5Desc: Mutual TLS enforcement for daemon communication. Certificate rotation, constant-time comparison, no weak ciphers.
security.layer6Desc: OCI image signatures verified on pull. Content-addressable storage prevents tampering. Digest pins for production.
security.rootlessDesc1: Doki was designed to run without privileges from day one. Unlike Docker, which requires root or a rootful daemon, Doki executes every container as an unprivileged user.
security.rootlessDesc2: On Android, this means no bootloader unlock. On Linux, no sudo in CI pipelines. On macOS, no privileged helper tools.
security.rootlessDesc3: For defense-in-depth details, see the Security documentation.

performance.subtext: Doki is engineered for resource-constrained devices. Smaller binary, less memory, faster startup — without sacrificing compatibility.
performance.footnote: Benchmarks measured on a Pixel 8 (Android 14, 8GB RAM). Docker measured on Ubuntu 24.04 (x86_64, 16GB RAM). Your mileage may vary.
